title: trichera communis subsp. sylvatica
intro: Trichera communis subsp. sylvatica, also known as Wild Bergamot, is a subspecies of the Trichera communis plant that is native to North America. This plant is valued for its fragrant leaves and flowers, which have been used in herbal medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Wild Bergamot include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Wild Bergamot include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, and it can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried herb for consumption.
